reason: Both findings concern the absence of `W11BB03SearchForm` from hints arrays. This is a sample-application-specific class name used only to illustrate the pattern in documentation examples — it is not a Nablarch framework API. No real user would search for this class name when looking for guidance on dynamic IN/ORDER BY clause generation. All meaningful retrieval terms (ParameterizedSqlPStatement, IN句 動的生成, $if, $sort, sortId, kengenKbn, etc.) are already present in the hints. The omission has zero impact on retrieval quality. Phase E did not apply the fix because the fix agent correctly judged (implicitly) that adding a sample-only class name to hints provides no retrieval value, leaving the output unchanged from the input.

findings_assessment.justification: W11BB03SearchForm is a sample-only class name from the documentation example, not a Nablarch framework API. Users seeking guidance on dynamic IN clause generation would search using terms like ParameterizedSqlPStatement, IN句 動的生成, kengenKbn, List, $if — all of which are already in s1.hints. No user would query by this sample class name, so its absence does not degrade retrieval quality.

W11BB03SearchForm is the same sample class reused for the ORDER BY dynamic change example. Users seeking guidance on dynamic ORDER BY would search using sortId, $sort, ORDER BY 動的変更, ParameterizedSqlPStatement — all present in s2.hints. The class name itself carries no retrieval signal for this topic.
